title: FigureRequestV1
description:
What a caller AUTHORS. This is the complete set of fields a caller is permitted to set.

The defining rule of the contract: a caller declares what its data IS. It never declares what Cortexel concluded about it. There is deliberately no field here for a validation result, a reference-comparison status, an accessibility conformance claim, a completeness assertion, a paper-reproduction claim, a calibrated-posterior claim, an output digest, or the text of a mandatory disclosure. Those are library-generated facts and live only in FigureArtifactV1. Sending one is not ignored — it is rejected with PROVENANCE_CALLER_ASSURANCE_FORBIDDEN.

This envelope validates the shape common to every skill and lets the router read `skill.id` before dispatch. The per-skill schemas under contract/schemas/skills/ are self-contained and are the actual acceptance authority for `data` and `parameters`.

$schema.description: Optional. A versioned schema identifier. A stored artifact must never reference a mutable `latest` URL.

contract.description: Which contract this request is written against. Required: an undeclared contract is not a 1.0 request.

contractDigest.description: Optional. If present it MUST equal the installed build's contract digest, otherwise CONTRACT_DIGEST_MISMATCH. This lets an agent pin the exact contract it validated against; it can never be used to assert a digest Cortexel does not have.

revision.description: Optional in an authored request. Omit to accept the installed revision. A revision this build does not provide is refused before canonicalization rather than approximated. After successful resolution, Cortexel always materializes the resolved installed revision at canonicalRequest.skill.revision, so an omitted pin and an explicit-current pin have identical canonical bytes and request digest.

parameters.description: How to analyse and present the observations. Closed per skill. There is no `.passthrough()`: a typo in a scientific parameter must fail rather than be silently dropped.
